summary.what: Expand default skip-rule set and fix exact-path matching bug
summary.why: Default-deny pending checks were nagging on housekeeping files (gitignore, editorconfig, narrowly-scoped .github/ metadata) that carry zero design intent — relaxing them by default eliminates ~10-15% of friction with no opt-in.
summary.how: Replaced prefix-only infrastructurePrefixes with a typed skipRule (prefix/exact/suffix) in skiprules.go; the new exact-path semantics also fixes a latent strings.HasPrefix bug where '.gitignore' would match '.gitignores'.

notes: Design review pushed back on .github/ as a directory (workflows/ are substantive) and on CHANGELOG.md (project-specific convention — belongs in user .timbersignore, not built-ins). .claude/ also dropped — slash commands are substantive. Kept the exact-path file added to the matcher because Bead 2 (.timbersignore) will reuse the same rule types; splitting into skiprules.go now avoids reshuffling next change.
